Course Title: AI in Clinical Decisions: When “Helpful” Becomes Harmful
Category: Healthcare & Life Sciences: Medical Services, Pharmacy, Biotechnology, Hospitals
Who is this for: Healthcare professionals, doctors, nurses, physician assistants, nurse practitioners, clinical staff, medical technicians, pharmacists, healthcare administrators
Team or Dept this is for: Clinical Operations, Medical Affairs, Patient Safety, Quality Assurance, Risk Management, Healthcare IT, Nursing, Pharmacy
Description: Risks of relying on AI for diagnosis or treatment recommendations.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with everyday healthcare settings and a general curiosity about how digital tools are being used in clinical practice are all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key risks associated with using AI tools for clinical diagnosis and treatment recommendations, and explain how these risks can affect patient safety in real-world healthcare settings. You will also be able to apply practical strategies to critically evaluate AI-generated clinical suggestions, ensuring that human judgement and professional accountability remain central to every patient care decision.
